sources.label: StockTitan - Vontier lifts buyback authorization to $1.0B (May 19, pre-window)
Businesswire - Vontier Q1 2026 results and reaffirmed FY26 guidance (May 7, pre-window)
Motley Fool - Vontier Q1 2026 earnings transcript (Teletrac divestiture, $220M, June close expected)

verdicts.rankRationale: No new evidence since June 6 on the latent edge-data thesis; +0.74% today is drift and yesterday's verdict already reconciled the -24% YTD setup. The pending Teletrac telematics sale (announced pre-window) is a known watch item, not fresh information.
verdicts.bull: Beaten-down -24% YTD with a $1.0B buyback authorization on a $4.0B cap gives a hard floor while the fueling/retail device-network edge-data option stays free; any AI/edge framing by management would be entirely unpriced.
verdicts.neutral: Vontier remains a sleepy GDP-ish industrial with a speculative, non-consensus AI angle; until management monetizes device data, the name trades on fueling capex and buyback math, not the thesis.
verdicts.bear: Selling Teletrac removes one of the clearest connected-device data assets, weakening the edge-data narrative this page rests on; core fueling exposure faces secular EV-transition pressure.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Teletrac divestiture close in June plus aggressive buyback deployment; any management framing of diagnostics/connected-device data as an edge-AI asset.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Portfolio refocus strips out the data-network assets the latent-AI thesis depends on, leaving a shrinking fueling-hardware core.

verdicts.rankRationale: Strict-screen pathway (mobility/sensing/fueling/environmental supporting energy-managed industrial campuses) is unchanged; no contracts, guidance, or capacity news since June 6 and the adjusted-score rank already sits well ahead of displayed rank per yesterday's reconciliation.
verdicts.bull: FY26 guidance reaffirmed ($3.10-3.15B sales, ~3% core growth) with margin expansion intact and most of FY26 FCF going to buybacks; the energy-managed-campus pathway costs nothing at this valuation after a -24% YTD reset.
verdicts.neutral: Evidence on the strict pathway is static; the stock is a capital-return story near-term and the industrial-campus energy angle remains a slow-burn option with no new proof points this week.
verdicts.bear: Low single-digit core growth and EV-driven erosion of fueling infrastructure demand cap the upside; divestitures shrink the sensing/telematics footprint the strict screen credits.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 results and Teletrac close confirming cleaner portfolio plus buyback acceleration; EV-charging/fleet-infrastructure wins that validate the energy-management pathway.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Core fueling demand decay outpaces the pivot, making the strict-screen pathway a narrative without revenue.
